QF.id: SEBI Guidelines for AML Standards, CFT and PF with KYC Norms_16

question.text: Which of the following cash transaction records must be maintained by registered intermediaries under Rule 3 of PML Rules?

answer.text: All cash transactions of the value of more than Rs. 10 lakh or its equivalent in foreign currency
All cash transactions of the value of more than Rs. 5 lakh
All cash transactions of the value of more than Rs. 50 lakh
All cash transactions of the value of more than Rs. 1 lakh

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Under Rule 3 of PML Rules, intermediaries must maintain records of: (i) all cash transactions above Rs. 10 lakh or equivalent in foreign currency, (ii) series of connected cash transactions below Rs. 10 lakh each but aggregating above Rs. 10 lakh within a month, (iii) transactions involving forged/counterfeit currency, and (iv) all suspicious transactions.
**The Trap:** The threshold is Rs. 10 lakh, not Rs. 5 lakh or Rs. 50 lakh. This is a commonly tested figure. Also note that connected transactions below the threshold but aggregating above it within a month must ALSO be recorded.
**Cross-Exam Context:** The Rs. 10 lakh threshold appears in PMLA rules and is tested across all NISM AML modules. It is the standard cash transaction reporting trigger for all reporting entities in India.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Even though securities transactions are mostly electronic, cash dealings can occur in some contexts. Any cash transaction above Rs. 10 lakh triggers mandatory record-keeping. Watch for structured transactions designed to stay below this threshold -- that itself is suspicious.
